steps.title: Identify the clause structures
steps.content:
Left side of the blank: “One such type of matter is a form of cooled, condensed gas called a Bose-Einstein condensate (BEC)” is an independent clause.

Right side: “Dutch physicist Lene Hau famously used a BEC to slow a beam of light to a complete halt” is also an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining independent clauses
steps.content: A semicolon joins two independent clauses correctly. The other choices violate this rule by using insufficient punctuation—either a comma without a conjunction, a conjunction without its required comma, or no punctuation at all. That confirms C is correct.
